name: Orcs Must Die: By The Blade

reviews.review_description: It's a fun brawler with a little strategy with setting traps.  I honestly didn't think it would be for me but I picked it up on the bundle deal and I am having a blast.  Only 3 levels in but need to go back to the first level and find all the chest and bonus room.  Definitely recommend this.  I am surprised it doesn't get more attention.  I hope they keep it updated and maybe do some holiday themes🤞

reviews.review_title: Good game and great update thx :D
reviews.review_description:
I’d like to update my previous review following the recent patch.

First of all, thank you for continuing to work on the game. You have my full support 🙂

The game isn’t perfect yet, especially when it comes to combat. Sometimes hits still don’t properly register on enemies, which can be frustrating (I’ve lost several runs because of that). But you are clearly working on it and you’ve shown that you’re aware the system still needs improvement, which is very appreciated.

Performance has also improved a lot. At first, the game was stuttering so badly on my setup that it was almost unplayable. That’s no longer the case. Aside from a few slowdowns right after loading, the game now runs smoothly and looks great. Thanks for that fix 😀

The interface is also much less intrusive during combat, which makes the overall experience much better.

In my opinion, this is a good game with a really nice atmosphere. It still needs a few adjustments to make combat less frustrating, but overall, as long as the developers continue supporting and improving the game, that’s a very positive sign.

Regarding weapon durability and your idea:

"In the future, we plan to introduce a weapon priming system, where using a repair station grants a small buff."

I think that’s a really good idea 🙂

Thanks again for the continued work and improvements.

And if one day you could add a French translation, that would be amazing. Playing only in English can be a bit difficult when it’s not your native language, and it’s easy to miss some nuances.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for updating your review, it really makes a big difference to us! We're glad to hear you're enjoying the game more and that you like our idea for the weapon buff.

We're already hard at work on the second patch and look forward to sharing more details soon.

We hope you continue to have fun!

reviews.review_description:
This game is very underwhelming in its current state. I like the original OMD games and was looking very forward to this one, but it is unpolished and shouldn't have released in this poor state.

Here are some of my pain points:
- Combat is not very enjoyable, slashes phase through the enemies or you get completely body blocked.
- Only snap-turning. There is an option for smooth turning, but you can't set it for now (says coming soon), the fact that it didn't launch with something so important is completely beyond me.
- Audio bugs, try listening to the orcs and you often hear them cut off or general sounds sound like plastic. It sounds really cheap.
- The inventory/holster is a pain, whenever your hand is remotely in front of your stomach/chest it gets triggered which causes tons of issues when fighting (failed to use the healing trinket several times because of this bs).
- Why durability on weapons and why make them drain so quickly, too? It is really annoying having to run back and forth to a whetstone while a million orcs swarm a stupid chest.
- Talking about chests, this needs to be addressed as well, it is so stupid that the orcs only focus the chest and ignore the player, no way to kite them away or anything.
- Multiplayer not working, tried joining a friend and he tried joining me. Maybe servers are having problems, no idea.

There was probably more which I can't recall right now. I would highly recommend waiting for this game to receive substantial updates before even consider buying it. I'll reconsider updating my review in the future, but this is basically an early access release right now.

developer_response.body:
Hi Zanktus,

Thanks again for your review, we really appreciate the time you took to share your feedback. We wanted to let you know that a new patch has been released, addressing many of the issues you raised. Here’s a quick overview of what’s been updated:

- Combat improvements have been implemented, with further refinements planned in upcoming patches.

- Smooth turning has been added.

- Audio issues have been addressed. However, if you encounter any further problems, please let us know.

- The inventory UI has been adjusted to be less intrusive. We’re happy to continue refining it based on player feedback.

- Weapon durability has been removed. While we liked the concept during development, we recognize it wasn’t resonating with players. We’re exploring alternative mechanics, including a potential whetstone buff in a future update.

- Enemy behavior has been improved. Orcs should now engage with players more actively instead of focusing solely on the chest. (As always, we welcome further feedback.)

- Multiplayer issues have been resolved.

We hope you’ll find the time to jump back in and give the game another try. If you do, we’d greatly appreciate it if you considered updating your review based on the latest version.

All the best!
Teravision Games

reviews.review_description: I give this game all five stars because it’s fun and chaotic  and I just love when I get my war hammers I just bash there heads in and I love the story part of it and you get a chance to upgrade your weapons and your traps anybody that’s on the fence should pick this up you won’t regret it I promise you that and thanks to the devs that made this game they are very awesome keep up with all the updates I been playing all night since I got the game can’t get enough of it.￼

Orcs must die is a long running franchise that I had never actually come across until now. After a bit of research I soon realised that it was a tower defense style series where you lay traps to stop the onslaught of Orcs destroying the town.

This is the first venture for the series into VR and puts more emphasis on hand-to-hand combat, along with the traps.  I've yet to complete the game but I have been enjoying what I've played so far, and ultimately that's what gaming should be right? Fun!

For single player there's plenty of content to keep you hacking and slashing away for hours but the fun is more than doubled when you bring a friend along for the journey!

There are a few niggling issues here and there at the moment BUT I have 100% faith in Teravision Games to address these and support the future of the game. They aren't the kind of Devs that just want to cash in and move on! Oh and it's at a very competitive price to boot!

Give the game a chance and leave the Orcs no chance but to DIE BY THE BLADE!!!
😁✌️☮️❤️

developer_response.body:
Thank you Hippy Pickle! Reading that you are having fun makes us incredibly happy. In order to improve immersion in VR, we intentionally made this game is very different from the original OMDs in terms of mechanics, while being super authentic to their comical tone and beautiful atmosphere.  Hopefully this title encourages you to try out the original (non-VR) games, which are fantastic!  

There are a bunch of improvements that we already implemented at our end, but we couldn't include them in the launch version because of technicalities in the publishing process. That said, there is a first update coming live very soon! 

On top of that, we're currently working very hard on additional updates, so you're absolutely right to assume that our job isn't done yet with this beloved game of ours.

reviews.review_title: Great Time Taking Down Orcs With Humor
reviews.review_description:
This game turns orc-slaying into a full-on VR workout — and I’m not even mad about it. Every swing, slice, and wild flail feels gloriously chaotic, like mixing medieval combat with accidental yoga. The physics are crunchy, the orcs are loud, and victory sounds like pure satisfaction.
If you’ve ever wanted to feel like a sweaty hero in a fantasy apocalypse, this is your game. Just keep water nearby — and maybe warn your furniture. 💪🪓

This game makes me laugh, grit my teeth like a warrior, shout a victory cry, and laugh again. I’m really enjoying it. Yes, it has a few minor launch bugs and tweaks that need to be made, but if you follow TeraVision on social they’ve been very transparent about the patches that they’re working on. I really appreciate that honesty. Can’t wait to jump back in and play some more!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much, BoKnowsGaming! We’re thrilled to hear you’re having a great time.

We truly appreciate you recognizing our clear and transparent approach to communication. Keeping our players informed about updates, patches, and fixes is incredibly important to us, and your feedback plays a big role in shaping the experience.

We’re excited to roll out the upcoming patch and can’t wait to hear what you think. Thanks for being part of the journey!

reviews.review_title: Why can’t people just have fun
reviews.review_description: Man it ain’t perfect but it ain’t  bad long as you can have fun that’s all that matters I thought I mean dang coming from the snes and genesis days you learn to appreciate the what gaming has evolved too I’m just saying keep up the good work with some updates just have fun people why does everything have to be so technical!!!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks Onedeep5484! Glad to read that you are having fun. We agree that it's not perfect, and definitely we want to make it better, so we are updating the game very soon with a bunch of improvements. We love our game, and we are not done with it!

reviews.review_description:
If you love fantasy world, in the World of Warcraft visual style - this game is for you. The combat is well done, lots to do and the game is just fun overall. 
I was slightly annoyed by the loading screens, but as you get into the game more and more, you don’t mind them.

I played other orcs must die games on pc years ago and I aware that it's an aracady game so I don't expect blades and sorcery level combat... But the game is not smooth and feels sluggish... Some swings at the orcs pass right through their hit boxes... and the audio of them grunting or being damaged starts to glitch out or cut out completely when swarmed with more than one orc while hitting them... And the lack of smooth turning on launch is a big weird question mark for me... Why?.... Plus the holster system is bad and I hate the fact that every time I hover over my chest for half a second the layout of the inventory appears in front of my face... During fights I would swing and it would still appear... Not enough time to place traps.... The durability system feels like a chore... I hate how fast weapons lose durability especially ones you pick up from the orcs...

developer_response.body:
Hi Speeko107,

Thank you for your feedback, we really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. We wanted to let you know that a new patch has been released, addressing many of the issues you raised. Here’s a summary of the improvements:

- Performance has been improved.

- Combat has been updated, with further refinements planned in future patches.

- Audio glitches and cut-outs have been fixed. (If you encounter any further issues, please let us know.)

- Smooth turning is now available.

- The inventory UI has been adjusted to be less intrusive, and we’ll continue refining it based on player feedback.

- Timing has been adjusted to give players more preparation before the horde arrives.

- Weapon durability has been removed. We’re currently exploring alternative mechanics, including a temporary weapon buff using the whetstone in a future update.

We hope you’ll have the chance to jump back in and experience these improvements. If you feel the changes have enhanced the game, we’d greatly appreciate you considering updating your review.

Thanks again, your feedback truly helped us improve the experience.

All the best,
Teravision Games

reviews.review_description:
I'm just out of the tutorial and into the first couple of levels. I like the art style and it feels like Deadpool in that way. 

I like that levels give you a since of exploring to achieve your objectives. Tho I think it might need to emphasize the importance of that book into the tutorial more. 

Combat needs a little work as far as parry and hit boxes. 

You can see that the game is using space warp heavily. Most In the menu. 

The inventory system needs work grabbing weapons don't always work as fast as it should. 

Feedback from hits and passing Thur objects needs work as well. 

Seems like the food laying around we be used for in game health instead of set pieces. Or the books give a boost or hit or something as well.

developer_response.body:
Thanks, SaoGalaxy! Super happy to read that you are enjoying the game. There are a bunch of improvements that we already implemented at our end, but we couldn't include in the launch version because of some technicalities within the publishing process. That said, there is a 1st update patch coming very soon! 

On top of that, we are currently working very hard on improving the inventory and hit collisions, among other stuff. Thanks for helping us make the game better with your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Enjoying the game- the patch has definitely improved the performance. Combat is ok but needs some refinement regarding blocking and parrying .
The developer seems to be reactive to feedback and hard at work with patches

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing, we really appreciate it!

We're carefully taking in all the feedback we've received and will continue improving the game.

You mentioned that the combat could use some refinement, and we're happy to share that we've already started working on improvements in this area.

reviews.review_title: Looks very promising but needs 4 players
reviews.review_description: 2 player co-op is nice but our gaming nights with quest are us and another married couple.  We play Demeo, Golf, Dungeons of Eternity and Elven Assassins.  All have 4 player support.  This REALLY needs 4 player support to be a huge hit.

The game is fun to play. But make the weapons to be upgraded instead of having to  sharpen them. Also the weapons you fined in the level can be switched out with the main weaopn that located on the back. The whole point of this game is to kill orcs not to run around sharpening your weapons. Instead, make the player use coins to upgrade the weapon to increase strength.

Running around is fun but leads to motion sickness a lot, and that ruins the fun of the game #1 reason people don't play long on vr. My solution for you, the creator of this game. Give people an option to use teleportation to move a round. L joy stick move the character when fighting close combat. R joy stick to teleport around the level to reduce motion sickness. A good example would be dungeon of Eternity. 

I can play that game with ZERO motion sickness do to teleportation movement using the joystick.

Those holsters are really annoying. Grabbing your weapons from the holsters is an exercise in patience. That parrying needs work, seems to be hit or miss. Plus there's stutter at the start of levels, probably from shader compilation. And I guess performance later on in more crowded battles can be an issue. Not quite ready for release, but the game itself is fun. There's hope.

developer_response.body:
Hi namekuseijin,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback, we really appreciate your support at launch.

We’re happy to let you know that our first patch has been released, addressing many of the issues you raised. Here’s a quick summary of the updates:

- The inventory UI is now less intrusive, and we’re happy to continue refining it based on player feedback.

- Parrying and blocking are currently being reworked and will be included in a future patch. In the meantime, overall combat has already seen improvements.

- Overall performance has been improved.

We truly appreciate you playing at release and leaving a review. We hope you’ll give the game another try and check out the latest update. If you feel the experience has improved, we’d be grateful if you considered updating your review.

All the best,
Teravision Games

reviews.review_description:
This game really surprised me. While it has some polishing left to do, it hooked me in a way no other VR game has before. After the first few levels, once rewards unlock, you start figuring out which traps work best in which scenarios  and that's when the real fun begins. I'd love to see an even more hardcore difficulty mode; that would be awesome. Personally, I love the melee combat, but the trap strategy is what really got me hooked. From a VR perspective as a strategy game lover, this combo hit just right for me. The AI blew me away super dynamic, with a combat manager that adapts to the situation. The bosses felt fun, and I couldn't beat any of them on the first try. Shoutout to the AI team on this one.
Great job overall to the devs can't wait for the next update!

After patch 1, multiplayer works now.
Good and fun game to play with friends.
Please add more new missions in the future updates.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for updating your review after the new patch, it really means a lot to us! We're really glad to hear you're now enjoying multiplayer and having fun with the game.

reviews.review_description: A lot of fun! Combat is solid and laying traps is the best part! Sharpening weapons at the grind stones is unique and not at all cumbersome. It also has Quest Achievements!!!
i like it, it is funny game  , but i can not hurt boss,  i stop at donjon's stairs

￼￼ the games combat  is so glitchy that you might as well give up on fighting with weapons unless you’re swing is extremely slow 

￼ Constant clipping through enemies and not dealing damage 

￼ i’ve uninstalled and removed this game in 30 minutes 
￼￼

developer_response.body:
Hey Atlamus,

Thanks for taking the time to share your feedback with us, we really appreciate it.

We’re currently reworking the combat system based on player input, and Patch 2 is coming very soon. This update will include improvements to collision, blocking, and parrying, which we hope will create a smoother and more responsive experience.

We know this doesn’t immediately resolve your concerns, but we’d love for you to give the game another try once the update is live. If you feel the changes improve your experience, you’re more than welcome to update your review.

Thanks again for your support and for helping us improve the game.

Best regards,
Teravision Games

reviews.review_description:
First I want to start off by saying I love the Orcs Must Die series, and I was excited to hear it was getting a VR release, so It’s incredibly disappointing how this game turned out. It feels so thrown together. My biggest complaints are as follows. 

1. The UI is janky and covers everything you do in VR and not in a good way. You can’t see your hands or anything in front of you because massive walls of test are covering the screen. It’s almost like the VR portion took a back burner. 

2. The game has this weird pass-through like motion blur when moving like the early days of mixed reality hand tracking, EXCEPT this isn’t a passthrough game or uses hand tracking so it’s so odd and immersion breaking. 

3. Wonky or lack of collision of objects. Some items you pick up or hit just phase through you, have no collision at all or have collision with absolutely everything in a non smooth way that just become annoying. Be prepared for you to not be able to swing your sword properly because it’s snagged on your massive hands or for boxes not to break and you just sit there looking awkward because you didn’t swing the controller at Mach speed.  

Gameplay would be decent if all of these things weren’t an issue. If they can ever fix all of this, there is a game here. Orcs Must Die is still in the DNA somewhere, and it does have some pretty interesting mechanics.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, Justicetheninja! There is a bunch of improvements that we have already implemented at our end, but couldn't include them in the launch version because of some technicalities in the publishing process. We are also working very hard at this very moments on additional tweaks on the UI front and weapon collisions, among other things. Hopefully you stick around, give those updates a try and let us know what you think!

reviews.review_title: Not very exciting - want a refund
reviews.review_description:
I am a big fan of the orcs must die series. Played it since i was 15 years old. Was happy to see this game on the store but i sadly only played it for 2 hours.

First the points i have to praise: 

- the game is not laggy or buggy anymore like the other reviews said (yeah sometimes the hit of your weapon does not do any damage and "phases" through but not a big deal)

-It is very immersive 

- the weapon arsenal room and coop mode is a nice and new feature

Now the (sadly) negative points:

- MY F****** GAME CRASHED MID GAME. THATS UNACCEPTABLE (i got the metaquest 3)

- for all OMD series fans: the game is not like the old ones. The story mode is more linear than "level" like.

- The tutorial is unnecessary long and the story mode is boring af. I played for 2 hours and i forced myself to play till my game crashed. 

- maps are too small and boring

- no save option mid game? Just dumb checkpoints?
Orcs Must Die: By The Blade is a very fun take on the classic tower defense games. Lots of wacky moments and hilarious with friends.

developer_response.body: Thank you! We are really glad that you appreciate our interpretation of the OMD games, which we admire very much.  Ours is intentionally very different from the originals, aiming at making the experience way more immersive for VR.

reviews.review_description: The fighting mechanism is very basis or has some issues. When fighting, it’s basically hack and slash all day with some traps. You dont feel the attacks and they just go through the orcs. When hitting boxes, the sword would a lot of times just go through or bounce off. It feels very flimsy and weightless. When dropping and regrabbing the swords, you sometimes will grab with them upside down. Even when they’re facing upright on the ground. When parrying with orcs, it’s like there’s only one sweet spot where the sword has to be there or doesn’t parry. Even when you visually see your sword  parrying their weapon. When it comes to guarding the chest, they just beeline straight to it and ignore you. So you can’t strategically kite them around traps. Besides these minor inconvenies, a fun humorous fantasy adventure.i think the studio really home in on this being a very funny game by giving the orcs more humors sayings and antics. In terms of combat experience, this is where the game disappoints. I’d wait till the game goes on discount cuz I dont feel like I’m getting enough of an experience to justify $19. This feels more like a $11-$14 game.

reviews.review_title: Orc meat is back on the menu bois
reviews.review_description: I'm a huge Orcs Must Die fan and it's pretty awesome that we got a new one in VR. So far so good, i haven't had any issues.

developer_response.body: Thanks, IndiGhost199X! We really put a lot of love & effort on capturing the original OMD atmosphere, while making mechanics intentionally different to enhance immersion in VR. There are some updates coming soon!

reviews.review_description: good
Stuck on 4 i raise my hand to the rift and nothing happens

developer_response.body:
Thanks for supporting the game and taking the time to leave a review!

We're sorry to hear you've run into this issue. The good news is that our team is already working on a fix, so hopefully you'll be able to get back to battling the Orcs very soon!

reviews.review_description: The learning curve was a bit rough.  But once you figure it out great game.

developer_response.body: Thank you, TwoScoopz007! Love to hear you like the game. There is an update with improvement coming live pretty soon.

reviews.review_description: Fun game. Please look into the crashes on 1-2.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you are having fun, Majed6! There are a bunch of improvements that we already implemented, but couldn't add into the launch version because of some technicalities in the publishing process. That said, a first update is coming very soon!

reviews.review_title: VR rebuild that struggles to deliver
reviews.review_description: Orcs Must Die: By The Blade has introduced a somewhat popular franchise to virtual reality, but sadly, it currently falls a bit short. Although they have successfully captured every aspect of the game and rebuilt it specifically for VR, there are inconsistencies throughout. For instance, traps occasionally fail to activate, and the blocking and parrying mechanics often struggle to register, which will discourage players from utilising this part of the combat system. When combined, these issues make the core gameplay feel lacklustre, preventing me from becoming fully immersed and engaged in the experience. I believe that if they can address these problems and enhance the combat feel, the game could truly shine, especially considering its well-implemented upgrade systems. Unfortunately, the overall presentation is marred by bugs, such as an overly sensitive inventory system, awkward enemy animations, audio dropouts, and repetitive quips that lose their humour over time. This resulted in an experience that failed to keep me entertained beyond brief pick-up and play sessions.

developer_response.body:
Thanks, VRRealm! We are really happy to hear that you appreciate the upgrade systems! We invested a lot of time on creating a meaningful amount of content for you to collect, unlock and upgrade. This is not a small game!

There is a bunch of improvements, including audio fixes, that we already have implemented on our end, that unfortunately couldn't include in the launch version because some technicalities with the publishing process. But we are very excited to share that a 1st update with those will go live very soon!

On top of that, we are currently working on some other tweaks that include the inventory system, hit collisions, among many other things. Hope you stick around and let us know what you think. 

Thanks so much for helping us make our game better by investing your precious time on sharing your feedback with us!

reviews.review_description: Great game, hope there will be future updates?

developer_response.body: Thanks CalibanDan! Yes! There is a bunch of improvements that we already have implemented but we couldn't add in the launch version because of some technicalities with the publishing process. That said, our 1st patch is coming live very soon... and we are currently working on more!

reviews.review_title: A Great Game Made Even Better by Update
reviews.review_description: The melee combat is now incredibly weighty, responsive, and satisfying. There’s a huge variety of weapons and traps, and the cartoon-fantasy environments look gorgeous in VR. It's pure, frantic hack-and-slash fun from start to finish. Plus, by playing this, you're supporting an incredibly talented Latin American studio. Highly recommend. Update 1 made a massive difference
How do you play co op?  Don’t want to play and not be able to refund. Did the tutorial. Where is co op?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for purchasing Orcs Must Die: By the Blade, we truly appreciate your support!

Co-op mode unlocks after completing both the tutorial and Level 1-1.

Our team is currently hard at work on our first patch, which will include a range of improvements to the experience.

We’re excited to keep building and refining the game, and we hope you’ll stick with us on the journey.

reviews.review_description:
Echoing other early reviews. There's a base for something fun but it needs some work to make it more intuitive to play. It's clunky and frustrating and should have been held back a bit longer. Weapon quality is a big problem, inventory is irritating and combat is literally hit and miss, especially when locked to snap turning. There's something odd going on with weapon pickups and storage. It wouldn't let me store the hammer it guided me to pick up. Let me pick up the hammer multiple times, and somehow I ended up with two swords, one of which it wouldn't let me repair. 

Net result is it was more of a chore than fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks for reaching out, Krykoyd! We are glad that you see the fun potential of what we are going for. There are a bunch of improvements already implemented on our side, but because of technicalities in the publishing process, we couldn’t include those in the launch version. That said, an update is coming very soon in which, for example, you will find the smooth turning implemented. We are also removing weapon deterioration and polishing the storage apparition rules. Hope you stick around and let us know what you think about this 1st batch of improvements. We really appreciate how your feedback helps us make the game reach its full potential!

reviews.review_title: Orcs Must Die! Again and again.Always!
reviews.review_description: Big time awesome here. A fun experience that is well-crafted in one of my favorite series. Definitely recommend the game! Fun will be had!

developer_response.body: Thanks, Randygbk! There are updates coming live very soon.

reviews.review_description: I play VR with my family including my father who is 70.  Please add comfort settings like teleportation for motion sickness.  We can't the game without it.  We were really looking forward to this game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the interest in our game! We currently have snap turn and corner vignettes options to help with motion sickness. We are also adding in our very-soon-to-be-live 1st update, smooth turning for people who prefer to have continuous movement. Being transparent, we used to have teleportation during development, but we had to remove it because we quickly found out that our testers would abused it to cheat out from Orcs fights.
